description_long: This is a slender, upright flowering plant with a single main stem that branches slightly and supports several clusters of pale lavender to white tubular flowers. The flowers are arranged near the upper portion of the stem, each with a distinct, elongated corolla and a slightly flared opening. The leaves are green, moderately sized, and have a rounded, slightly lobed or scalloped margin, attached alternately along the stem. The stem itself is thin, green, and appears somewhat woody or fibrous at the base, with a few visible nodes. The overall structure is delicate, with the flowers and leaves spaced apart, giving the plant an airy appearance. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of decorative landscaping.
description: A slender, upright plant with pale lavender tubular flowers and rounded green leaves on a thin stem. The flowers cluster near the top, giving it a delicate, airy look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main stem, several leaves, and a prominent cluster of pale flowers at the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated flowers and the alternate arrangement of leaves along the stem.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear look at the stem's structure and the back of the flower cluster, as well as more visible leaf shapes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the flower cluster from a different angle and the lower stem's curvature.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a bilateral (zygomorphic) configuration, forming a tubular corolla with a slightly flared, two-lipped opening. The petals overlap slightly at the base and are fused into a tube, with symmetry along one plane.
